mappings.evidence_note: Compulsory topic 'Earth': planet composition, separation methods, tests for water, carbonate reactions.
Compulsory topic 'Microscopic World I': atomic structure, periodic table, bonding (ionic/covalent/metallic), structure-property relationships, formulae.
Compulsory topic 'Metals': occurrence, reactivity series, extraction, corrosion, moles/stoichiometry, reactions.
Compulsory topic 'Acids and Bases': acid/base properties, indicators, pH, molarity, titrations, salt preparation.
Compulsory topic 'Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds': hydrocarbons, fractional distillation, alkanes/alkenes, alcohols/carboxylic acids, polymers.
Compulsory topic 'Microscopic World II': electronegativity, polar bonds, molecular polarity, intermolecular forces, hydrogen bonding, giant covalent structures.
Compulsory topic 'Redox, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis': oxidation numbers, redox reactions, simple cells, electrolysis, electroplating.
Compulsory topic 'Chemical Reactions and Energy': enthalpy change, calorimetry, Hess's law; bomb calorimetry out of scope.
Compulsory topic 'Reaction Rate': methods to follow reactions, factors affecting rate (concentration, temperature, surface area, catalyst), molar volume of gas.
Compulsory topic 'Chemical Equilibrium': dynamic equilibrium, Kc, temperature/concentration effects; inert species and acid-base/redox equilibrium extensions out of scope.
Compulsory topic 'Chemistry of Carbon Compounds': homologous series, isomerism, reactions of functional groups, polymers (addition/condensation).
Compulsory topic 'Patterns in the Chemical World': periodic trends (period 3 physical properties, oxide acid-base character), transition metals.
Elective topic 'Industrial Chemistry': rate equations, activation energy, catalysis, Haber process, chlor-alkali, methanol synthesis, green chemistry.
Elective topic 'Materials Chemistry': natural/synthetic polymers, polymer processing, metals/alloys, liquid crystals, nanomaterials, green chemistry.
Elective topic 'Analytical Chemistry': chemical tests, separation/purification, volumetric analysis, colorimetry, IR spectroscopy, mass spectrometry.
Paper 1 covers compulsory part, 60%, 2h30m.
Paper 2 covers elective part (2 of 3 topics), 20%, 1h.
School-based assessment (SBA) 20%; assesses practical and inquiry skills.
Public exam uses MCQ, short questions, structured questions, essay questions.
Assessment objectives: recall, experimental technique, data handling, application, communication, evaluation.

draft_notes.note: No explicit difficulty taxonomy in source; inferred from question-style descriptions on p.123.
Topic 16 is curriculum time for investigative study; it supports SBA but is not a public-exam content topic.
